name: RansomwareAttack
description: A case template for handling suspected or confirmed ransomware incidents, including triage, containment, investigation, eradication, recovery, communications, and post-incident activities.
author: Zachary Carter
summary: Suspected or confirmed ransomware incident affecting [business unit / systems]. Initial activity identified on [date/time]. Current scope includes [hosts/accounts/data]. Suspected initial access vector: [unknown/phishing/VPN/RDP/etc.]. Current status: [triage/contained/eradication/recovery]. Business impact: [summary].

tasks.title: Validate ransomware activity
tasks.description: Confirm whether ransomware activity is present by identifying file encryption, ransom notes, destructive actions, service disruption, extortion messaging, or other supporting evidence. Determine whether the case is confirmed ransomware, attempted ransomware, suspicious activity, or a false positive.

tasks.title: Initiate incident communications and escalation
tasks.description: Notify required internal stakeholders and activate the appropriate incident response, management, legal, privacy, communications, and executive escalation paths based on case severity and business impact.

tasks.title: Identify affected systems, accounts, and business impact
tasks.description: Determine the scope of the incident by identifying affected hosts, user accounts, servers, shares, applications, backup systems, and business functions. Document confirmed and suspected impact.

tasks.title: Preserve forensic evidence
tasks.description: Collect and preserve volatile and non-volatile evidence from affected and suspected systems, including memory, disk artifacts, ransom notes, binaries, scripts, event logs, authentication logs, EDR telemetry, network telemetry, scheduled tasks, services, and persistence artifacts.

tasks.title: Contain hosts, accounts, and network access
tasks.description: Isolate affected or suspected systems, disable or restrict compromised accounts, revoke sessions, disable remote access paths as needed, and block malicious or suspicious infrastructure to prevent further spread.

tasks.title: Identify initial access vector
tasks.description: Investigate how the threat actor gained access, such as phishing, exposed VPN, RDP, credential compromise, software vulnerability exploitation, malicious downloads, or third-party access.

tasks.title: Investigate privilege escalation and lateral movement
tasks.description: Determine how the threat actor elevated privileges, moved through the environment, accessed additional systems, and expanded control across users, hosts, shares, or management infrastructure.

tasks.title: Assess data exfiltration and extortion risk
tasks.description: Determine whether data was staged, compressed, transferred, or exposed prior to encryption. Identify sensitive data types, possible destinations, extortion indicators, and any leak-site or ransom communication references.

tasks.title: Analyze ransomware artifacts and behavior
tasks.description: Analyze ransomware binaries, scripts, ransom notes, file extensions, process execution, command lines, persistence mechanisms, and related artifacts. Identify the ransomware family or variant if possible.

tasks.title: Extract, validate, and track IOCs
tasks.description: Extract and validate indicators of compromise from malware artifacts, host evidence, network telemetry, and external intelligence sources. Add confirmed IOCs to the case, tag appropriately, and share with downstream systems as authorized.

tasks.title: Eradicate persistence and unauthorized access
tasks.description: Remove malicious binaries, scripts, scheduled tasks, services, persistence mechanisms, unauthorized accounts, and attacker tools. Reset compromised credentials, revoke tokens, and remediate exposed services or exploited vulnerabilities.

tasks.title: Validate backup integrity and recovery readiness
tasks.description: Confirm that backups are clean, available, and uncompromised. Identify safe restore points and verify that containment, credential remediation, and persistence removal are sufficiently complete before restoration begins.

tasks.title: Restore systems and data
tasks.description: Restore affected systems, services, and data from validated clean backups or rebuilt systems. Validate restored functionality, monitor for reinfection, and ensure recovery actions are documented.

tasks.title: Assess external reporting and notification obligations
tasks.description: Determine whether law enforcement, regulators, cyber insurance, customers, partners, or other external parties must be notified based on contractual, legal, regulatory, or policy requirements.

tasks.title: Harden controls and increase monitoring
tasks.description: Implement security improvements identified during the investigation, including MFA enforcement, segmentation, credential hygiene, logging improvements, detection coverage, patching, backup protection, and monitoring enhancements.

tasks.title: Conduct lessons learned and close case
tasks.description: Document root cause, response actions, business impact, key findings, and improvement opportunities. Confirm recovery is complete, no ongoing malicious activity is observed, required notifications are resolved, and closure criteria are met.

notes.title: Initial summary
notes.content: High-level summary of the suspected or confirmed ransomware incident, including when it was identified and why the case was opened.

notes.title: Current status
notes.content: Current phase of response, key blockers, and most recent operational update.

notes.title: Business impact
notes.content: Summary of impacted business units, services, users, systems, and operational disruption.

notes.title: Decision log
notes.content: Document key response decisions, approvals, and major turning points during the incident.

notes.title: Initial access
notes.content: Document the suspected or confirmed initial access vector and supporting evidence.

notes.title: Privilege escalation and lateral movement
notes.content: Track evidence of credential abuse, privilege escalation, remote execution, and lateral movement.

notes.title: Persistence
notes.content: Document persistence mechanisms, attacker footholds, and related artifacts.

notes.title: Exfiltration findings
notes.content: Summarize evidence of data staging, compression, transfer, extortion, and possible data exposure.

notes.title: Ransomware variant and behavior
notes.content: Document ransomware family identification, execution behavior, ransom note details, file extensions, and related TTPs.

notes.title: IOC summary
notes.content: Summarize validated indicators of compromise and relevant intelligence context.

notes.title: Affected hosts
notes.content: List confirmed and suspected affected workstations, servers, VMs, and infrastructure.

notes.title: Affected accounts
notes.content: Document impacted user, service, admin, and privileged accounts.

notes.title: Affected data and shares
notes.content: Track impacted file shares, storage locations, sensitive data, and affected repositories.

notes.title: Backup systems status
notes.content: Document the status of backup systems, backup integrity, and restore-point availability.

notes.title: Containment actions
notes.content: Record host isolation, network blocks, account disablement, session revocation, and other containment steps.

notes.title: Credential remediation
notes.content: Track password resets, token revocations, MFA changes, and privileged access remediation.

notes.title: Blocking and prevention actions
notes.content: Document firewall blocks, EDR blocks, deny lists, segmentation changes, and control improvements implemented during response.

notes.title: Eradication actions
notes.content: Summarize malware removal, persistence removal, tooling cleanup, and vulnerability remediation.

notes.title: Backup validation
notes.content: Document how backups were assessed for integrity and selected for recovery.

notes.title: Restore actions
notes.content: Track systems restored, recovery sequence, exceptions, and issues encountered during restoration.

notes.title: Validation testing
notes.content: Summarize post-restore validation, monitoring results, and checks for re-encryption or ongoing malicious activity.

notes.title: Residual risk
notes.content: Document any remaining risk, unresolved findings, compensating controls, or follow-up actions.

notes.title: Internal stakeholder communications
notes.content: Summarize communications with incident responders, IT, leadership, and other internal stakeholders.

notes.title: Executive updates
notes.content: Track executive-facing summaries, decisions, approvals, and reporting cadence.

notes.title: Legal, privacy, and compliance coordination
notes.content: Document discussions related to legal review, privacy impact, contractual obligations, and compliance considerations.

notes.title: External communications
notes.content: Record communications with law enforcement, regulators, insurance, customers, vendors, or partners as applicable.

notes.title: Threat actor communication log
notes.content: Document ransom notes, email messages, portal communications, or any interaction with the threat actor, if applicable.

notes.title: Root cause
notes.content: Summarize the suspected or confirmed root cause of the incident and supporting evidence.

notes.title: Lessons learned
notes.content: Document lessons learned, process gaps, technical findings, and recommended improvements.

notes.title: Control improvement plan
notes.content: Track recommended security improvements, ownership, and follow-up actions after case closure.
